title: 578. 100 minuter rymdaktier
summary_md:
# 578. 100 minuter rymdaktier

**Feed**: sparpodden  
**Published**: 2026-06-15  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A 100-minute special on space stocks featuring a former space-fund manager (Magnus Niklasson) and a space-tech researcher now at Unibap (Anders Persson). Themes: satellites dominate the sector (communications via Starlink, GPS/positioning, and the fast-growing Earth Observation segment), falling launch costs via SpaceX driving commercialization, and SpaceX's IPO bringing institutional capital into the sector. The researcher is notably skeptical of SpaceX's space-datacenter and lunar/Mars ambitions on physics and economics grounds, while acknowledging SpaceX's transformative role.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 SPACEX — SpaceX | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

IPO Friday; valuation rich on 1-yr view but possibly cheap on 5-10 yr. Bull case is unique self-contained AI/launch ecosystem; bear case is unproven space-datacenter and Mars economics.

#### Reasoning

Niklasson frames SpaceX as transformative and the entry point pulling institutional capital into space; potential Apple-like ecosystem across launch, xAI/Grok, TerraFab chips and connectivity. But much of the valuation rests on future visions (1M datacenter satellites, lunar/Mars) that depend on Starship success and Musk's delivery record. Persson is skeptical of space-datacenter physics (poor radiative-only cooling, costly data transfer). View it on a 10-year horizon.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Starship fails to reach reliable reusable service ~2027-2028
- if space-datacenter economics prove unviable
- if institutional capital inflow disappoints post-IPO
- if Musk fails to deliver on key milestones

> *"jag tycker man ska se det väldigt långsiktigt tio års tid det här kanske är en dyr aktie på ett års tid men på fem års tid så kanske det är billigt"*

---

### 🟠 UNIBAP — Unibap | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Stockholm-listed Uppsala space company doing onboard AI image analysis on satellites, targeting defense ISR needs (fast, minutes-level intelligence). Guest is its strategy/product chief and discloses bias.

#### Reasoning

Persson argues the key bottleneck in Earth Observation is getting data down from orbit cheaply and fast; Unibap's approach runs AI analysis onboard and sends only the result (a text message), valuable for defense that prizes speed. Promising tech in a growing segment, but presented by a biased insider with no explicit recommendation, so treat as a speculative watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if onboard-compute demand fails to materialize commercially
- if defense procurement does not scale
- if competitors commoditize onboard AI processing

> *"det är ju någonting som mitt företag sysslar väldigt mycket med för att kunna göra analysen ombord på satelliten"*

---

### 🟠 BKSY — BlackSky Technologies | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Cited as a leading US Earth Observation player; EO framed as the sector's next big commercial breakthrough, currently driven mainly by defense demand.

#### Reasoning

Hosts identify Earth Observation as the fourth and emerging commercial breakthrough in space (after broadcasting, positioning, communications). Falling launch costs and AI for processing imagery widen the addressable market. BlackSky is named as a high-resolution EO example, but the segment still lacks broad paying customers, so it is a thematic watch rather than a conviction call.

#### Sell Triggers

- if EO fails to find a large commercial use case beyond defense
- if paying customer base does not broaden
- if data-downlink costs remain prohibitive

> *"Black Sky Technologies i USA som fokuserar mycket på högupplösta bilder på specifika områden"*

---

### 🟠 PL — Planet Labs | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

US EO company aiming for daily live global imagery (a live Google Earth); cited within the EO theme the hosts see as the next commercial wave.

#### Reasoning

Planet Labs is highlighted for its ambition to image the whole Earth daily. The hosts see EO as the fastest-growing satellite sub-segment and the next breakthrough, enabled by cheaper launch and AI processing. But monetization is unproven and demand today is mostly defense, so this is a speculative thematic watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if daily-coverage ambition fails to convert to revenue
- if commercial demand stays thin
- if EO economics do not improve with launch costs

> *"Planet Labs till exempel, de jobbar de har en ambition att de vill kunna varje dag ha en live-uppdatering av hela jorden"*

---

## Summary Table

| Ticker | Company | Signal | TH | Conf | Context |
|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **SPACEX** | SpaceX | 🟠 WATCH | LT | 50% | IPO Friday; valuation rich on 1-yr view but possibly cheap on 5-10 yr. Bull case is unique self-contained AI/launch ecosystem; bear case is unproven space-datacenter and Mars economics. |
| **UNIBAP** | Unibap | 🟠 WATCH | LT | 40% | Stockholm-listed Uppsala space company doing onboard AI image analysis on satellites, targeting defense ISR needs (fast, minutes-level intelligence). Guest is its strategy/product chief and discloses bias. |
| **BKSY** | BlackSky Technologies | 🟠 WATCH | LT | 40% | Cited as a leading US Earth Observation player; EO framed as the sector's next big commercial breakthrough, currently driven mainly by defense demand. |
| **PL** | Planet Labs | 🟠 WATCH | LT | 40% | US EO company aiming for daily live global imagery (a live Google Earth); cited within the EO theme the hosts see as the next commercial wave. |
